page: Amazon Web Services c8gd.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. Operating at 2.8 GHz, this instance provides 32 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, backed by 64 KB L1d cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 36 MB L3 cache. The hardware configuration includes 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. Local storage is provided via a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD instance store, and network connectivity is rated at a 15 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark data highlights strong memory subsystem performance, with large-block read-only memory bandwidth reaching 384.64 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 170.56 GB/sec, both placing in the top tier. The instance also demonstrates top-tier performance in multi-core processing, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 21489.0 and a Passmark database operations score of 46924.96. Single-core performance and cryptographic operations fall within average ranges, while software compilation and bzip3 decompression show strong multi-threaded capabilities.

The 2.0 GB of RAM per core represents a potential bottleneck for memory-intensive applications, positioning this instance primarily for compute-bound tasks. Additionally, while the instance handles small and medium LLM inference text generation at average to strong speeds, it exhibits weak performance in prompt processing for large 70B models, yielding 5.91 tokens/sec. Given the combination of dedicated ARM64 cores, high-speed local NVMe storage, and strong database benchmark results, this instance is suited for distributed databases, web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded data decompression workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.8xlarge is a compute-optimized server powered by a 32-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor running at 2.8 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of system memory, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and includes a bundled 1900 GB NVMe SSD local instance store alongside a 15 Gbps baseline network. Benchmark results indicate top-tier memory bandwidth, with large-block reads at 384.64 GB/sec, and strong database operations. However, the low memory-to-core ratio and weak performance in large 70B LLM prompt processing represent notable tradeoffs. This instance is suited for compute-intensive workloads, multi-threaded software compilation, web serving, and local-storage-dependent database applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8gd.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized server featuring 32 dedicated Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and a 1900 GB local NVMe SSD, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth and database performance.
